finding: reduced internal consistency reduced motivation to acquire external attributes; low self-other distinction suppresses a preference-expansion effect
a non-verbal cue (digital smile marker) decreased perceived competence and reduced information-sharing behavior, moderated by context formality
less exposure to historical evidence predicted denial of systemic bias; an intervention providing historical evidence increased acknowledgment of systemic bias
greater perceived continuity with a future self predicted higher well-being 10 years later, controlling for initial well-being
clinically diagnosed disorder group showed faster disengagement from threat cues, opposite to the pattern in subclinical samples
cognitive capacity moderated whether an expectation-discrepancy generated assimilation or contrast in affective judgment
identity status (developmental stage) correlated with personality dimensions; achievement status involved low neuroticism and high conscientiousness

combining two intervention types (external-focus cue plus augmented feedback) produced superior motor performance with reduced muscular activation; a third intervention type (monetary reward) added no further benefit
elevated expectations (manipulated exogenously) increased effort provision in a real task, consistent with expectation-based reference dependence
liability for harm reduced market inefficiency; reputation had little effect; competition reduced prices but did not improve efficiency without liability
workers responded to delayed-wage commitment by supplying sufficient effort; reciprocity and social preferences mediated the effect beyond monetary incentives alone
asset markets preceding a coordination game reduced efficiency outcomes despite no direct distortion of game incentives; market signals forecast game outcomes
communication effectiveness in information-asymmetry settings depended on feasibility of low-ability agents joining a mutually beneficial outcome
salient group identity shifted equilibrium selection from inefficient to efficient coordination, mediated by learning dynamics
positive affect reduced time discounting (decreased preference for present over future consumption)
faster decision cycle (smaller time steps in interaction) increased cooperation rates in a dilemma; response capacity stabilizes mutual cooperation
in repeated interaction with implementation noise, lenient and forgiving strategies outperformed uncooperative strategies; strategic diversity persisted despite learning
two distinct job-design equilibria emerged endogenously; high-discretion high-wage jobs were viable only with pre-screening and labor-market competition
confusion (expectation of stable value given declining fundamentals) drove mispricing; contextualization (reframing assets as finite resources) reduced confusion and mispricing
a policy incentive (priority in allocation for prior registration) increased willingness to commit to a self-sacrificial action
